channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Raised ridge across lane, 26th June
item.description:
There was a raised ridge right across the outside lane of the anticlockwise carriageway. Suspect the heat had caused a joint in the tarmac to expand. Not nice to hit this at 70mph.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): M25, Epping Forest
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge pavement potholes, 26th June
item.description:
Pothole for 2 years 2 feet by 1 feet 1 inch deep Nearly break my ankle every time I step off my front yard Fix up Lambeth council!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St. Julian's Farm Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling left on pavement, 26th June
item.description:
At approximately 10:12pm on 26 June 2026, dog fouling was left on the pavement outside 29 Berryhill, and was not removed. I have CCTV footage showing the incident. The fouling presents a health hazard and has been left on the public footway - families with small children living nearby. Please arrange for it to be cleaned and investigate if appropriate. I can provide the CCTV footage if required.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Berryhill, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Business owner of Amitabha Garden of 10 Wood Wharf dumped shipping box on street, 26th June
item.description:
Commercial fly-tipping on Wood Wharf (SE10 9FL) by the business owner of Amitabha Garden (10 Wood Wharf), as evidenced by the attached photos of the dumped shipping box and its legible business shipping label.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Horseferry Place, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parking saving on road, not dropped kerb, and mud all over pavement being walked intomour houses, 26th June
item.description:
As above. Building work on our road where we have alot of parking required. Builders blocking at least 2 spaces all day and night so we cannot park anywhere near our house. They are leaving mud all over the pavement and road which is being walked into our houses. They are making excessive noise with trucks arriving before 8am.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cambridge Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sign vandalism, 26th June
item.description:
“Boundary Road” has been pulled from the ground and dumped in the garden of 31 Bexley Road who pulled it out to build a new brick wall for their extension. The extension is ongoing and the scaffolding for this is obstructing the pavement as you walk up Boundary Road. The street sign post has been like this for just over 2 weeks! Deliveries, cabs, emergency services and visitors can no longer find “Boundary Road” as the sign is dumped in their garden. I hope that they are fined for the vandalism of this as it is unacceptable for residents to have to end up paying for it to be put back in the ground!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Boundary Road, Falconwood, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big head up rubbish, 26th June
item.description:
Big load of household waste and furniture has been here for around 6 weeks now. Reported multiple times but different people. Still not dealt with and is now growing in size

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mallams Mews, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins, 26th June
item.description:
Clearly if you sign off many thousands of people arriving on a hot day. More bins or emptied more often.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crystal Palace Station Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 12 months since temporary manhole cover, 26th June
item.description:
A temporary yellow manhole cover on pavement. Second time reported but it has now been left for a WHOLE YEAR. This is unacceptable plus it has moved creating a more severe trip hazard than before as the manhole it is protecting is now exposed. Immediate action must be taken to repair manhole to remove this eyesore. It is located outside no. 3 Berkeley Avenue on the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Berkeley Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass verges are not being cut, 26th June
item.description:
The field is being cut but the grass verges are being left and are becoming over grown obscuring line of sight as you drive up the road and as this is next to a school children could step out from the field and not be seen.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tillingbourne Green, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, including food on pavement near bin store, 26th June
item.description:
Hard rubbish, litter and food on pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensbridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing abandoned commercial bin and fly tipping next to it, 26th June
item.description:
This non has been here for over 4 weeks and it’s being pushed from one spot to another. Now people started putting additional rubbish next to it and it’s a growing dump.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tabernacle Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged pavement, 26th June
item.description:
New paving stines buckled upwards

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn High Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 26th June
item.description:
Dead fox behind the wall in the garden of council estate on corner of Greenwich South Street and Blisset street
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Blissett Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branch hanging down over road., 26th June
item.description:
This happened today. Branch still attached to the tree.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Vale, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Table dumped (fly tip again), 26th June
item.description:
Thanks so much I don’t know why this is such a fly tipping hot spot! A table this time. Thanks for removing when you’re able to.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Page's Walk, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped between backgardens, 26th June
item.description:
Mixed rubbish, broken furniture and other litter dumped in no man’s land between gardens. This appeared in the last few days. Can be viewed from the back gardens of number 7 and number 9 Coxmount Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wolfe Crescent, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside Kingsgate Workshops, 110-116 Kingsgate Road, 26th June
item.description:
The Loading Only bay was full of Lime e bikes,(see photo) even though an e bike bay was available. This is the only Loading Only bay outside the Kingsgate Workshops.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsgate Road, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Averyhill park burnt out motor bike, 26th June
item.description:
Burnt out motor bike back of averyhill park. On pathway/cycle way. Field that backs on to charlton athletic practice ground.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sparrows Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown base of tree obstructing crossing and passing, 26th June
item.description:
Overgrown public tree that makes it very hard to cross the road as you cannot see oncoming vehicles there is also little to no access for a wheelchair user or someone in a pram.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burnt Ash Lane, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
